question_text: Asha is driving at 30 km/h on a straight road. Bharat is 45 metres behind Asha and driving in the same direction. Charu is driving along the same road from the opposite direction at 50 km/h and is currently 200 metres away from Asha. The speed, in km/h, at which Bharat should drive so that all three meet at the same instant is:

Two motorboats travel on a long, straight river segment at constant speeds. Starting at the same instant from different points, they take 1.5 hours to meet when travelling toward each other, and 7.5 hours to meet when travelling in the same direction. If the slower motorboat's speed is 40 km/h, then the distance, in km, that the slower motorboat covers when they meet while travelling toward each other is:

Veer takes 24 seconds to cross a moving walkway at an airport while walking on it in the direction of motion at his normal pace. If he walks at three times his normal pace in the same direction, he takes 12 seconds. The time, in seconds, that Veer would take to cross the walkway if he simply stood still on it is:

Ports M and N are 150 km apart along a straight sea route. Ferry F1 leaves M at 7:00 am heading toward N at 40 km/h, and ferry F2 leaves N at 8:30 am heading toward M at 20 km/h. The two ferries pass each other at:

The distance from harbour X to harbour Y is twice the distance from harbour W to harbour X. Two cargo ships travel from W to Y via X. While going from W to X, ship 2's speed is twice that of ship 1; while going from X to Y, their speeds are interchanged. The ratio of the total time taken by ship 1 to that taken by ship 2 in going from W to Y is:

Rama leaves home at the same time every morning to walk to the school bus stop. When he walks at 6 km/h he reaches the stop at 8:30 am, and when he walks at 10 km/h he reaches at 8:10 am. If on one morning he leaves home at 8:00 am, the speed, in km/h, at which he must walk to reach the bus stop exactly at 8:20 am is:

Two bicycles A and B have wheels of circumference 60 cm and 100 cm respectively. While covering a certain distance, each wheel of bicycle A made 4,000 more revolutions than each wheel of bicycle B. If bicycle B covered this distance in 30 minutes, its speed, in km/h, was:

Charu walks from town X to town Z via town Y along a straight route, and then runs back along the same route at a speed that is 25% higher than her walking speed. She takes exactly 2 hours to walk from Y to Z, and the same 2 hours to run from Y to X. The total time, in minutes, that she would take to walk from X to Y and run from Y to Z is:

A delivery van went on a 5-hour route. Before the route, the odometer reading x km was a palindrome (a whole number whose digits read the same forwards as backwards). At the end of the route, the odometer read 47974 km, which is also a palindrome. If the driver never exceeded 90 km/h during the route, the greatest possible average speed (in km/h) at which the driver drove during the route was:

Train T1 leaves city A heading toward city B at 48 km/h, and at the same instant train T2 leaves B heading toward A on the same track. After the two trains pass each other, T1 takes 4 hours to reach B while T2 takes 9 hours to reach A. The distance, in km, between A and B is:

Pooja drives to work every weekday morning, departing at 8:30 am and arriving exactly on time at her usual speed of 48 km/h. When she drives at 40 km/h instead, she is 6 minutes late. One morning, she covers two-thirds of the route in one-third of her usual total commute time, and then stops for 4 minutes. The speed, in km/h, at which she should drive the remaining distance to arrive exactly on time is:

Two motorcyclists set out for the same destination, the first leaving at 8:00 am and the second leaving at 9:00 am. They reach the destination at exactly the same time. If the first motorcyclist travelled for at least 5 hours, the highest possible percentage by which the second motorcyclist's speed could exceed the first's is:

Two athletes Veer and Aanya start from a point P on a circular running track at the same instant, with Veer running clockwise and Aanya anti-clockwise. They meet for the first time at 7:00 am, by which time Veer has covered 75% of the track. If Veer returns to P at 7:08 am, then Aanya returns to P at:

To travel from town A to town B, one can use any of three transport modes — walking at 10 km/h, cycling at 20 km/h, or auto-rickshaw at 30 km/h. Mohan uses each mode for one-third of his total travel time, while Anjali uses each mode for one-third of the total distance. The percentage by which Anjali's travel time exceeds Mohan's travel time is nearest to:

Bharat walks from point A to point B, leaving A at 7:00 am and reaching B at 8:00 am. Starting from 7:01 am, every minute an electric scooter leaves A heading toward B; in all, 48 such scooters reach B by 8:00 am. All scooters travel at the same constant speed. If Bharat had doubled his walking speed, how many scooters would have reached B by the time Bharat reached B?

Maya covers a 148 km route on her motorcycle in four stages, taking a total of 2 hours 10 minutes. Her speeds in the four stages form an arithmetic progression, and the times spent in the four stages form another arithmetic progression. If she covers the first stage at a speed of 800 metres per minute for 25 minutes, the distance, in metres, that she covers in the fourth stage is:

Tara begins her drive at 6:00 am at a constant speed that would let her reach her destination by exactly 12 noon. On her way, she stops for 15 minutes, and afterwards increases her speed by 4 km/h to still arrive on time. Had she instead stopped for 15 minutes longer (i.e., 30 minutes total), she would have had to increase her speed by 10 km/h to still arrive on time. Her initial speed, in km/h, was:

A long-distance tour coach departs at 9:00 am along a fixed daily route. One day it travelled at a constant 60 km/h and reached its destination 2 hours later than its scheduled arrival. The next day, the coach covered two-thirds of the route in one-third of its scheduled total travel time, and then covered the remaining part of the route at 45 km/h, arriving exactly on time. The scheduled arrival time of the coach is:

Two towns M and N are 60 km apart, connected by a straight road. Sahil jogs from M to N while Veer jogs from N to M, both starting at the same instant. They pass each other in exactly 2 hours. If Sahil reaches N exactly 3 hours after Veer reaches M, then Sahil's jogging speed, in km/h, is:

Points A, M, N, B lie on the same straight highway with M, N, B respectively 100 km, 150 km, and 200 km from A. Taxis 1 and 2 leave A at the same instant and head toward B. At the same instant, taxi 3 leaves B and heads toward A. Taxi 3 meets taxi 1 at N, and meets taxi 2 at M. If each taxi moves at a uniform speed, then the ratio of the speed of taxi 2 to that of taxi 1 is:
A delivery rider's daily fuel expense (in $\text{₹}$ hundred) for $x$ trips is given by $f(x) = x + \frac{49}{x}$. For positive real $x$, the minimum value of $f(x)$ is:
On a rectangular plot, a farmer plans to grow wheat on $a$ hectares and mustard on $b$ hectares, where $a, b > 0$ and $ab = 36$. The minimum value of $a + b$ is:
An investor splits $\text{₹}18$ lakh between two stocks, allocating $x$ lakh to Stock-1 and $y$ lakh to Stock-2 (with $x + y = 18$ and $x, y > 0$). The maximum possible value of $xy$ is:
A bookstore wants to fence off a rectangular floor display whose perimeter is exactly $100$ cm. The maximum possible area (in sq cm) of the display is:
A coaching institute models its normalised test-difficulty index by $f(x) = x^2 - 10x + 34$, where $x$ is a real parameter. The minimum value of $f(x)$ is:

A music school's monthly contribution margin (in $\text{₹}$ thousand) is given by $M(x) = 40 + 8x - x^2$, where $x$ is the number of extra weekend workshops scheduled. The maximum value of $M(x)$ for real $x$ is:
A gym charges members $x$ thousand rupees as a one-time joining fee and $y$ thousand rupees as the annual fee, with $x, y > 0$ and $xy = 144$. The minimum value of $x + y$ is:
A magazine publishes $x$ print copies and $y$ digital copies of an issue, with $x + y = 20$ (both positive reals). The minimum value of $x^2 + y^2$ is:
A small-batch manufacturer's per-batch cost (in $\text{₹}$ lakh) for producing $x$ tonnes is $C(x) = \frac{x^2 + 9}{x}$, with $x > 0$. The minimum cost (in $\text{₹}$ lakh) is:

A restaurant chain has five branches along a straight expressway at the kilometre-markers $2$, $5$, $8$, $13$ and $19$. A central commissary is to be located at kilometre-marker $x$ on the same expressway. The minimum value of the total distance $|x - 2| + |x - 5| + |x - 8| + |x - 13| + |x - 19|$ is:
A sports squad has $x$ batsmen, $y$ bowlers and $z$ all-rounders, all positive reals, with $x + y + z = 24$. The maximum value of the product $xyz$ is:

Two indoor sports clubs lie on a straight street at the kilometre-markers $3$ and $11$. For a coach standing at marker $x$ on the same street, define $D(x) = |x - 3| + |x - 11|$. The minimum value of $D(x)$ is:

Two siblings, Neeta and Geeta, together receive $\text{₹}30$ from a relative, where each receives a positive integer number of rupees. The maximum possible value of (Neeta's amount) $\times$ (Geeta's amount) is:

Two apartment complexes are located on a straight road at the kilometre-markers $3$ and $9$. For a real value $x$ (the marker of a candidate site), define $S(x) = (x - 3)^2 + (x - 9)^2$. The minimum value of $S(x)$ is:
A coffee shop's profit-rate function for selling $x$ extra-large cups (with $x > 0$) is $g(x) = \frac{x}{3x^2 + 7x + 12}$. The maximum value of $g(x)$ is:

A regional newspaper currently has $1000$ subscribers and charges $\text{₹}400$ per month per subscriber. For every $\text{₹}5$ decrease in the monthly fee, it gains $50$ new subscribers. The operational cost of serving $N$ subscribers is $C(N) = 100N + 20000$. The monthly fee (in $\text{₹}$) that maximises the newspaper's monthly profit is:

A co-working space provider has the demand function $p = 300 - 0.4x$, where $p$ is the monthly fee (in $\text{₹}$ hundred) per desk and $x$ is the number of desks rented. The total monthly cost is $C(x) = 60x + 20000$. The number of desks that maximises monthly profit is:

A tuition centre commissions a square-based open-top water tank made from a metal sheet of total area $2700$ sq cm (the sheet forms the base and the four side walls). The maximum possible volume (in cu cm) of the tank is:

A length of decorative cable of fixed length $L$ is cut into two pieces. One piece is bent into a circle and the other into a square. The ratio of the side of the square to the radius of the circle so that the total enclosed area is minimum, is:
Three travel-segment ratings $x$, $y$, $z$ (real numbers) for domestic, international and weekend tour categories satisfy $x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = 4$. The maximum value of $xy + yz + zx$ is:

The rate of water flow through three pumps X, Y and Z in a swimming pool maintenance setup is in the ratio 3 : 7 : 21. An empty pool can be filled completely by pump X alone in 15 hours. If all three pumps are used simultaneously to fill the empty pool, the time, in minutes, required to fill it completely is nearest to:

An aquarium tank is emptied every day at 8:00 am for cleaning. Immediately after, either pump P, or pump Q, or both, start filling it until the tank is full. On Monday, P alone finished filling at 6:00 pm. On Tuesday, Q alone finished filling at 2:00 pm. On Wednesday, P worked alone from 8:00 am until 1:00 pm, after which Q worked alone from 1:00 pm to 4:00 pm to finish filling. On Thursday, both pumps were used simultaneously throughout. At what time was the tank full on Thursday?

A garden pond has an inlet pipe and an overflow drain. With the drain closed, the inlet pipe alone fills the empty pond in 9 hours. With the drain open, the inlet pipe fills the empty pond in 12 hours. If only the drain is open and the pond is full, the time taken, in hours, for the pond to become half-full is:

A rainwater harvesting tank is being filled by four small inlet pumps and one large inlet pump. Each of the four small pumps works at three-fourths the rate of the large pump. If all five pumps work together, they fill the empty tank in what fraction of the time the large pump alone would have taken?

Two cars meet at an intersection. Immediately afterwards, one car drives due south and the other due west, both at constant speeds. Three hours later, they are 75 km apart. If the speed of one car is 5 km/h more than that of the other, then the speed, in km/h, of the slower car is:

Two passenger trains cross each other in 12 seconds when running in opposite directions along parallel tracks. The faster train is 180 m long and crosses a lamp post in 9 seconds. If the speed of the other train is 9 km/h less than that of the faster one, its length, in metres, is:

Two pipes P and Q are attached to an empty irrigation water tank. Pipe P fills the tank while pipe Q drains it. If P is opened at 9 am and Q at 11 am, the tank becomes full at 5 pm. Instead, if P is opened at 9 am and Q at noon, the tank becomes full at 4 pm. If Q is not opened at all, the time, in minutes, taken by P alone to fill the empty tank is:

A milk tank at a dairy has inlets of two types P and Q. All inlets of type P, when open, bring in milk at the same rate; all inlets of type Q, when open, bring in milk at the same rate. The empty tank is completely filled in 20 minutes if 8 inlets of type P and 6 inlets of type Q are open, and in 30 minutes if 4 inlets of type P and 12 inlets of type Q are open. In how many minutes will the empty tank get completely filled if 5 inlets of type P and 15 inlets of type Q are open?

A pharmaceutical plant has four reactor tanks W, X, Y and Z, each containing 1000 litres of a liquid intermediate. The intermediate is being pumped between tanks as follows:
 From W to X @ 20 litres/minute
 From Y to W @ 80 litres/minute
 From W to Z @ 20 litres/minute
 From Y to Z @ 40 litres/minute
 From X to Y @ 100 litres/minute
 From Z to X @ 100 litres/minute
 Which tank gets emptied first, and how long, in minutes, does it take to get empty after pumping starts?

Two freight trains P and Q were moving in opposite directions, their speeds being in the ratio 4 : 3. The front end of P crossed the rear end of Q 30 seconds after the front ends of the two trains had crossed each other. It took another 50 seconds for the rear ends of the trains to cross each other. The ratio of the length of train P to that of train Q is:

Two cyclists are riding along a road beside a railway track at respective speeds of 3 and 6 km per hour in the same direction. A train comes from behind them and crosses them in 60 and 75 seconds, respectively. The time, in seconds, taken by the train to cross an electric post is nearest to:

Train P leaves station M for station N at 8 am. Train Q, travelling at two-thirds the speed of P, leaves N for M at 10 am. The two trains pass each other at a point Z, where the distance MZ is two-thirds of the distance MN. How many hours does train P take for its journey from M to N?

A long-distance bus travelled a certain distance at a uniform speed. Had the speed been 10 km/h more, it would have needed 5 hours less. Had the speed been 10 km/h less, it would have needed 10 hours more. The distance, in km, travelled by the bus is:

Aanya and Bhavna can row a boat at 4 km/h and 8 km/h in still water, respectively. In a river flowing at constant velocity, Bhavna takes 36 minutes more to row 9 km upstream than to row the same distance downstream. If Aanya starts from a certain location in the river, goes upstream and returns downstream to the same location in a total of 2 hours, then the total distance, in km, that Aanya covers is:

An intercity bus travelled at one-third of its usual speed and hence reached its destination 20 minutes after the scheduled time. On its return journey, the bus initially travelled at its usual speed for 3 minutes but then stopped for 2 minutes due to a brief halt. The percentage by which the bus must now increase its usual speed for the rest of the journey so as to reach the destination at the scheduled time, is nearest to:

Two cargo ships are sailing toward a port along straight routes at constant speeds. Initially, the two ships and the port form an equilateral triangle with sides of length 30 km. When the slower ship has travelled 6 km, the triangle formed by the new positions of the two ships and the port becomes right-angled. When the faster ship reaches the port, the distance, in km, between the other ship and the port will be:

Two buses start at the same time from stations U and V, travelling toward each other at constant speeds. Bus B1 reaches station V exactly 21 minutes after starting; bus B2 takes 16 minutes to reach station U after meeting bus B1. The total time taken, in minutes, by bus B2 to travel from station V to station U is:

A boat takes 3 hours to travel downstream a river from port P to port Q, and 4 hours to return from Q to P. Another boat takes a total of 10 hours to travel from port Q to port P and return to port Q. If the speeds of the boats and the river are all constant, the time, in hours, taken by the slower boat to travel from port P to port Q is:

A chemical tank is fitted with pipes, some filling it and the rest draining it. All filling pipes fill at the same rate, and all draining pipes drain at the same rate. The empty tank gets completely filled in 3 hours when 6 filling and 5 draining pipes are open, but this time becomes 8 hours when 5 filling and 6 draining pipes are open. In how many hours will the empty tank get completely filled when one draining and two filling pipes are open?

A vat used for processing olive oil at a refinery has two inlet pumps P and R and one outlet drain Q. The drain Q empties the full vat in one hour less than the time taken by pump P to fill the empty vat. When P, R and Q are all running together, the empty vat is filled in two hours. When Q and R are running together with the vat empty and Q is shut off after one hour, pump R takes another one hour and fifteen minutes to fill the remaining vat. If P alone can fill the empty vat in less than five hours, the time taken, in minutes, by R alone to fill the empty vat is:

A coffee shop tracks daily sales of a new pastry. The average number of pastries sold per day over the first 6 days after launch is 45, and over the first 7 days is 48. On the 8th day, the shop sells 7 pastries more than on the 7th day. The average number of pastries sold per day from the 2nd day to the 8th day is 52. The number of pastries sold on the 1st day after launch is:

There are four numbers such that the average of the first two numbers is 2 more than the first number, the average of the first three numbers is 4 more than the average of the first two numbers, and the average of the first four numbers is 6 more than the average of the first three numbers. The difference between the largest and the smallest of the four numbers is:

A small cricket club has four players P, Q, R, and S. When a new player T is added to the squad, the average height of the squad increases by y cm. If instead of T, a different player U had been added, the average height of the squad would have decreased by 3y cm. Given that T is 40 cm taller than U, the value of y is:

In a city's annual rainfall dataset, 25% of the recorded stations are located in coastal zones, while the rest are inland. The total rainfall recorded across all coastal stations during the year is three-tenths of the total rainfall recorded across all stations in the city. The ratio of the average rainfall recorded at a coastal station to the average rainfall recorded at an inland station is:

The average price of mangoes in a fruit basket increases by 80 paise when a batch of new mangoes is added to the basket. If the average price of a new mango is ₹4 more than the average price of an original mango, then the ratio of the number of original mangoes to the number of new mangoes in the final basket is:

A small bakery sells pastries and wants to achieve an average monthly profit of ₹1,200 over the course of a year. For the first eight months, the bakery's monthly revenue was ₹15,000 and its monthly cost was ₹14,200. From the ninth month onward, the bakery's monthly cost will rise to ₹14,500. In order to meet its yearly profit target, the bakery's monthly revenue from the ninth month onward should be:
The average of four positive integers is 17. When a natural number n is added to the set, the average of the resulting five integers is an even integer. The minimum possible value of n is:
If x and y are non-negative real numbers such that 4x + y = 16, then the average of the maximum and minimum possible values of (3x + y) is:

In a regional spelling contest, 20 participants competed and the arithmetic mean of their scores was 60. Five of these participants tied for the top score with identical scores. If the scores of the remaining participants are distinct integers with the lowest being 35, then the maximum possible score of the top five participants is:

A basketball player has played a certain number of matches so far in the season, and 10 more matches are still to be played. If she scores a total of 1 basket over the next 10 matches, her overall season average will be 0.2 baskets per match. If instead she scores a total of 4 baskets over the next 10 matches, her overall season average will be 0.25 baskets per match. The number of matches she has played so far is:

A factory employs 6 supervisors and 24 workers, with an average monthly wage across all 30 employees of ₹40,000. The management increases each worker's wage by 25%, while supervisors' wages remain unchanged. As a result, the average monthly wage of all 30 employees rises by 16%. The average monthly wage of a supervisor, in rupees, is:

Three distinct real numbers have an average of 45. The smallest number is increased by 12 and the largest is decreased by 18, and the order of the three numbers remains unchanged. After this change, the new arithmetic mean is 3 less than the middle number, and the difference between the new largest and the new smallest numbers is 41. The largest number in the original set is:

In a music school, the average number of practice hours per week logged by students in the Piano batch and the Violin batch are 24 and 56 respectively. The number of students in the Piano batch is 8 fewer than the number of students in the Violin batch. If the average number of practice hours per week across all students of both batches combined is an integer, then the difference between the maximum and minimum possible number of students in the Piano batch is:

Petrol is sold for 4 consecutive weeks at prices of ₹80, ₹100, ₹120, and ₹150 per litre respectively. A motorist spends a fixed amount of money on petrol in each of the first two weeks and then spends double that amount on petrol in each of the next two weeks. The average price paid by the motorist, in rupees per litre, over the four weeks is closest to:

A musician performed at n + 3 concerts during a tour, and the average number of standing ovations she received per concert was 27. In the last three concerts, she received 30, 16, and 10 standing ovations respectively. At each of the first n concerts, the number of standing ovations she received was less than 30. The average number of standing ovations across those first n concerts was 28, and the smallest count of standing ovations at any of those n concerts was y. The smallest possible value of y is:
The average of 30 integers is 8. Among these 30 integers, exactly 20 do not exceed 8. What is the highest possible value of the average of these 20 integers?

A bookstore stocks 20 hardcover books, each priced at no less than ₹600, and at most 30 paperback books, each priced at less than ₹600. The average price across all books in the bookstore is ₹450. What is the largest possible average price, in rupees, of the paperback books?

Six athletes, including Riya, complete a 400-metre race in which finishing times are recorded in whole seconds between 40 and 90 (both inclusive). The average finishing time across all six athletes is 65 seconds, and exactly four of them finished in more than 61 seconds. If no two athletes had the same finishing time and Riya was the fastest among the six, then the difference between the highest and lowest possible finishing times of Riya is:

A factory tracks daily output across a series of working days. If the first 8 days are excluded, the average daily output across the remaining days increases by 1 unit relative to the overall average. If instead the last 8 days are excluded, the average daily output across the remaining days decreases by 2 units relative to the overall average. The average daily output across the first 8 days alone is 30 units, and across the last 8 days alone is 45 units. The total number of days the factory operated is:

Twelve years ago, the ages of the seven employees of a small consulting firm added up to 217 years. Four years later, the founding partner retired at the age of 55 and was replaced the same year by a fresh graduate aged 22. Three years after that, another senior employee left at age 55 and was replaced the same year by another fresh graduate aged 22. The current average age of the seven employees at the firm is nearest to:

A basketball player attempts two free throws. Each throw is successful with the same probability $p$, independently of the other. The probability that the player makes exactly one of the two throws equals the probability that the player makes neither throw. The value of $p$ is:

A box contains $30$ lottery cards numbered $1$ to $30$. Two cards are drawn at random without replacement. Given that the numbers on both drawn cards are multiples of $3$, the probability that the two numbers are coprime (that is, their highest common factor is $1$) is:

On a television quiz, a contestant knows the answer to a particular question with probability $\frac{3}{5}$. If she knows it she answers correctly for certain; if she does not know it she guesses uniformly among the $4$ given options. Given that she answered the question correctly, the probability that she actually knew the answer is:

An urn contains $4$ gold tokens and $6$ silver tokens. Tokens are drawn one by one at random without replacement until a gold token appears. The probability that exactly $2$ silver tokens are drawn before the first gold token is:

A jar contains $10$ biscuits, of which $4$ are chocolate-coated and $6$ are plain. Two biscuits are drawn at random without replacement. Given that at least one of the two drawn biscuits is chocolate-coated, the probability that both drawn biscuits are chocolate-coated is:

A fair die is rolled twice and the two numbers obtained are noted. Given that the product of the two numbers is divisible by $5$, the probability that the sum of the two numbers is greater than $7$ is:

Two archers, A and B, each shoot once at a target. Archer A hits the target with probability $\frac{1}{2}$ and archer B hits it with probability $\frac{1}{4}$, the two shots being independent. Given that the target was hit exactly once, the probability that it was archer A who hit it is:

A fruit basket contains $6$ guavas, $3$ mangoes and $1$ papaya. A fruit is drawn at random; it is put back into the basket only if it is not a guava (that is, it is left out only when it is a guava). A second fruit is then drawn. The probability that the second fruit drawn is a mango is:

A novelty die has $9$ faces numbered $1$ to $9$, each equally likely. It is rolled repeatedly until a number greater than $6$ appears, and then the rolling stops. Given that the number $2$ never appeared before the rolling stopped, the probability that the first number greater than $6$ obtained is $8$ is:

A tray holds $10$ pastries, of which $4$ are stale and $6$ are fresh. Three pastries are picked one by one at random without replacement. Given that the third pastry picked is stale, the probability that exactly one of the first two pastries picked was stale is:

A coin is biased so that it shows heads with probability $\frac{1}{3}$. It is tossed repeatedly until two consecutive tosses show the same face, at which point tossing stops. Given that the first toss showed heads, the probability that the tossing stops with two consecutive heads is:

Three technicians independently attempt to repair a faulty machine. They succeed with probabilities $\frac{2}{3}$, $\frac{1}{2}$ and $\frac{2}{5}$ respectively. Given that exactly two of the three technicians succeeded, the probability that the first technician was one of those who succeeded is:

A bag contains $5$ red and $3$ white marbles. Two marbles are transferred at random from this bag into an empty pouch. One marble is then drawn at random from the pouch and it turns out to be red. The probability that both transferred marbles were red is:
In a board game, three fair dice are rolled together. Given that the sum of the three numbers shown is $9$, the probability that at least one of the dice shows a $2$ is:

A spinner is divided into $5$ equal sectors numbered $1$ to $5$. The spinner is spun $4$ times and the number it lands on is recorded each time. The probability that the largest number recorded over the $4$ spins is exactly $4$ is:
A coin is tossed $7$ times and the sequence of heads and tails is recorded. The probability that the sequence contains exactly $3$ heads and no two heads occur on consecutive tosses is:

A vase contains $2$ roses, $4$ lilies and $4$ tulips. Three flowers are drawn at random without replacement. Given that at least one of the drawn flowers is a rose, the probability that the three flowers are of three different kinds is:

In a game of darts, a player hits the bullseye on each throw with probability $\frac{1}{3}$, independently of other throws. The player keeps throwing until the first bullseye is hit. The probability that the first bullseye is hit on an even-numbered throw is:

A child picks one of three gift bags at random. Bag A contains $2$ red and $2$ blue balloons, Bag B contains $1$ red and $2$ blue balloons, and Bag C contains $1$ red and $2$ blue balloons. The child draws one balloon from the chosen bag and it is red. The probability that the child had picked Bag B is:
A bag contains $14$ tokens numbered $1$ to $14$. Two tokens are drawn at random without replacement. Given that the product of the two numbers drawn is even, the probability that their sum is odd is:

A batch of freshly harvested tomatoes contains 95% water by weight, while sun-dried tomato slices contain 30% water by weight, with the remainder being solid matter. If 60 kg of fresh tomatoes are processed into sun-dried slices, the weight of the sun-dried slices obtained is:
options: 3 kg
5 kg
12 kg

question_text: A bonus pool was divided among four employees: Arun, Bina, Chetan, and Dipti. Arun received 25% of the total pool. Bina received 40% of the remaining amount. Dipti received 40% less than Arun. The ratio of the amounts received by Arun and Chetan is:

In a hospital, 15% of the staff work in the emergency department. The total monthly wages paid to the emergency-department staff amount to one-fourth of the total monthly wages paid to all staff at the hospital. The ratio of the average monthly wage of an emergency-department staff member to the average monthly wage of a non-emergency staff member is:

A salesperson has made 30 customer visits so far and has closed deals with 40% of them. If she closes deals with 60% of the remaining customer visits she has planned, her overall closure rate for the campaign will be 50%. If instead she closes deals with 80% of the remaining visits, the total number of deals she will close in the campaign is:

A bookstore has 540 books on display, each either a paperback or a hardcover. The number of bestseller paperbacks equals the number of bestseller hardcovers. If 25% of the paperbacks and 50% of the hardcovers are bestsellers, then the number of books that are not bestsellers is:

Identical bath soaps are sold in jars of two sizes, regular and family. The family jar is sold for twice the price of the regular jar. If the selling price per gram of soap in the family jar is 10% less than that in the regular jar, then the percentage by which the weight of soap in the family jar exceeds that in the regular jar is nearest to:

In an office, 70% of the employees are full-time staff and the rest are interns. There are 40 more full-time staff than interns. If 80% of the employees, including 24 interns, attended the annual conference, the percentage of full-time staff who did NOT attend the conference is:

A freelance editor earns ₹100 per hour for routine editing work and ₹300 per hour for urgent rush jobs. In a particular month, she worked a total of 220 hours, and her income from rush jobs was 30% of her income from routine editing. The number of hours she spent on rush jobs that month was:
In a recent test series, P scored 20% less than Q, Q scored 25% more than R, and R scored 20% less than S. If P scored 96, then the score of S was:

A bakery sold a particular variety of pastry across four consecutive months. The number of pastries sold in months 1, 2, 3, and 4 were 100, 120, 140, and 160 respectively. The cost of producing each pastry was ₹60, and a fixed marked price was used throughout. Discounts of 20%, 10%, and 5% were offered on the marked price in months 1, 2, and 3 respectively, while no discount was offered in month 4. If the total profit across the four months was ₹26,520, then the marked price per pastry, in rupees, was:

A theatre group had more than 12 male actors and a certain number of female actors enrolled at the start of a season. After 75% of the male actors and 25% of the female actors left during the season, the remaining number of female actors was 9 more than the remaining number of male actors. The minimum possible number of actors initially enrolled in the group was:

In a workshop attended by 200 participants, the percentage of women was at least 40% and at most 60%. The rest of the participants were men. Each participant signed up for at least one of two breakout sessions: design thinking, data analytics, or both. Among the men, 60% signed up for design thinking and 60% signed up for data analytics. Among the women, 80% signed up for design thinking and 70% signed up for data analytics. The minimum and maximum possible number of participants who signed up for both sessions are:
options: 60 and 72
62 and 74
64 and 76
66 and 78

question_text: In April, the monthly tuition revenues earned by three coaching centres — Alpha, Beta and Gamma — are in the ratio 10 ∶ 8 ∶ 7. The three centres jointly pay rent for a shared office space, contributing 20%, 25%, and 30% of their respective revenues that month to cover the total rent. In May, the office rent remains unchanged, while the revenues of Alpha, Beta, and Gamma rise by 5%, 8%, and 10% respectively. In May, the percentage of their combined revenue that will be paid as office rent is nearest to:

The annual revenues of three boutique cafés — Reva, Tara, and Saira — are initially in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4, respectively. In the first year, their revenues grow by 25%, 20%, and 25% respectively. In the second year, Reva's revenue grows by 40% and Saira's revenue grows by 10%, while Tara's revenue grows to become equal to the mean revenue of the three cafés that year. Tara's revenue growth in the second year is:

In an election for the post of secretary in a residential society, four candidates contested, and 75% of the registered members cast their votes. One of the candidates received 30% of the cast votes, while the remaining three candidates received the remaining cast votes in the ratio 1 : 2 : 3. The winner received 270 votes more than the candidate with the second-highest votes. The number of registered members in the society was:

The total student enrolment of a coaching institute (across regular and online streams combined) grew by 30% from 2018 to 2020. Over the same period, regular-stream enrolment grew by 50% while online-stream enrolment grew by 20%. From 2020 to 2022, online-stream enrolment grew by 50%. In 2022, online-stream enrolment was three times the regular-stream enrolment. The percentage increase in the total student enrolment of the institute from 2018 to 2022 is:

A fruit syrup containing water and pure mango pulp is to be diluted in two ways. If a certain quantity of this original syrup is mixed with 5 kg of pure mango pulp, the resulting syrup contains 70% mango pulp by weight. If the same quantity of the original syrup is mixed with 5 kg of another syrup containing 60% mango pulp by weight, the resulting syrup contains 50% mango pulp by weight. The quantity of the original syrup, in kg, is:

The strength of a saline solution in percentage equals the grams of salt per 100 cc of water. Two 600 cc bottles are filled with saline solutions of strengths 45% and 15% respectively. A portion of the solution from the first bottle is poured out and replaced with an equal volume from the second bottle. If the strength of the solution in the first bottle becomes 35%, the volume, in cc, of the solution left in the second bottle is:

Two fruit juice concentrates, P and Q, are mixed in the ratio 1 : 3 by volume. The volume of the mixture is then doubled by adding concentrate P such that the resulting mixture contains 65% fruit pulp. If concentrate P contains 80% fruit pulp, the percentage of fruit pulp in concentrate Q is:

The concentration of a sugar syrup is q% if 100 ml of the syrup contains q grams of sugar. Each of three jars X, Y, Z contains 500 ml of sugar syrup at concentrations 10%, 22%, and 38% respectively. First, 100 ml of the syrup in jar X is transferred to jar Y. Next, 100 ml of the syrup in jar Y is transferred to jar Z. Finally, 100 ml of the syrup in jar Z is transferred to jar X. The concentration of the resulting syrup in jar X, in percentage, is:
A triangular sail $ABC$ is cut so that the two equal edges satisfy $AB = AC = 25$ cm and the base $BC = 30$ cm. The sum of the lengths, in cm, of all three altitudes of triangle $ABC$ is:

On a survey grid, three corners of a parallelogram-shaped plot $PQRS$ are recorded as $P(-5, -1)$, $Q(-2, -6)$ and $R(9, -3)$. The diagonal $SQ$ meets the $x$-axis at the point $(a, 0)$. The value of $a$ is:
A triangular flower bed has its three corners at the grid points $(3, 2)$, $(15, 2)$ and $(3, 11)$. The radius of the incircle of this triangle is:

A circular lawn is divided into two regions by a straight path that is a chord of length $14\sqrt{2}$ m. The chord subtends an angle of $90^\circ$ at the centre of the lawn. The area, in sq. m, of the smaller of the two regions is:
A rectangular courtyard $ABCD$ has $AB = 40$ m and $BC = 21$ m. $E$ is the midpoint of side $CD$. The radius, in m, of the incircle of triangle $ADE$ is:

Two straight walkways are drawn from a point $P$ outside a circular fountain with centre $O$, touching the boundary at $Q$ and $R$. Points $A$ and $B$ lie on $PQ$ and $PR$ respectively, so that the segment $AB$ is also a tangent to the fountain. If $\angle AOB = 65^\circ$, then $\angle APB$, in degrees, equals:
options: $50^\circ$
$65^\circ$
$40^\circ$
$25^\circ$

question_text: A trapezoidal window pane $ABCD$ has $AB$ parallel to $DC$, with $AD$ perpendicular to $AB$ and $AB = 2\cdot DC$. A circle inscribed in the pane touches all four sides and has radius $4$ cm. The area, in sq. cm, of the window pane $ABCD$ is:

A circular lake has centre $C$ and radius $5\sqrt{2}$ km. Two straight parallel jetty-lines $PQ$ and $SR$ are chords of the lake lying on opposite sides of a diameter. If $\angle PQC = 45^\circ$ and the perpendicular distances of $PQ$ and $SR$ from $C$ are in the ratio $5 : 1$, then the area, in sq. km, of quadrilateral $PQRS$ is:

On a triangular plot of farmland $ABC$, $AB = AC = 15$ m and $D$ is a point on side $BC$ with $AD = 9$ m. The segment $AD$ is extended to a point $E$ such that $\angle ACB = \angle AEB$. The length, in m, of $AE$ is:
A rhombus-shaped kite has each side $25$ cm long and an area of $336$ sq. cm. The absolute value of the difference between the lengths, in cm, of the two diagonals of the kite is:

Three identical circular coins are placed flat on a table so that each one touches the other two. A small coin $Y$ is set in the gap between them touching all three, and a large ring $X$ is drawn around them touching all three from outside. If the radius of $X$ exceeds that of $Y$, the ratio of the radius of $X$ to the radius of $Y$ is:

A trapezoidal billboard $ABCD$ has $AB$ parallel to $CD$. The slanted edges $AD$ and $BC$, when extended upward, meet at a point $E$. Given $AB = 3$ m, $CD = 1$ m and the perimeter of $ABCD$ is $10$ m, the perimeter, in m, of triangle $AEB$ is:

On a regular hexagonal floor tile $ABCDEF$, point $P$ lies on edge $AB$ with $AP : PB = 1 : 2$, and point $Q$ lies on edge $CD$ with $CQ : QD = 2 : 1$. The ratio of the area of trapezium $PBCQ$ to the area of the hexagon $ABCDEF$ is:

Three footpaths cross a triangular plaza $ABC$. Point $D$ lies on $BC$ and point $E$ lies on $AC$; the paths $AD$ and $BE$ cross at $T$ with $AD : AT = 3 : 2$ and $BE : BT = 4 : 3$. A fourth path $DF$, with $F$ on $AC$, is laid parallel to $BE$. The ratio $BD : CD$ is:
A regular octagonal tabletop $ABCDEFGH$ has each side of length $10$ cm. The area, in sq. cm, of the square $ACEG$ is:

A triangular metal plate $ABC$ has area $2304$ sq cm. $M$, $N$ and $P$ are the midpoints of sides $AB$, $BC$ and $AC$ respectively. The medians of the plate drawn from $A$, $B$ and $C$ meet the segments $MP$, $MN$ and $NP$ at $X$, $Y$ and $Z$ respectively. The area, in sq cm, of triangle $XYZ$ is:

A closed rectangular gift box is placed snugly inside a spherical ornament so that all eight corners of the box touch the sphere. The box has total surface area $252$ sq cm and the sum of the lengths of all its edges is $96$ cm. The volume, in cubic cm, of the spherical ornament is:

A triangular pennant is inscribed in a circular emblem of radius $R$ so that one side of the pennant is a diameter of the emblem and the other two sides are in the ratio $p : q$. The area of the triangular pennant is:

A circular radar zone $C$ is described by the equation $x^2 + y^2 - 4x - 8y + 11 = 0$. Let $L$ be the locus of all points from which the pair of tangents drawn to $C$ meet at an angle of $60^\circ$. The point at which $L$ touches the line $x = 8$ is:

An isosceles roof truss is modelled by triangle $ABC$ with $AB = AC$. $AD$ is the altitude from $A$ to $BC$, and $BE$ is the altitude from $B$ to $AC$; these two altitudes cross at $O$ with $\angle AOB = 105^\circ$. The ratio $\dfrac{AD}{BE}$ equals:
options: $\frac{\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{2}}{2}$
$\frac{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{2}}{4}$
$\frac{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{2}}{2}$
$\sqrt{2}$

question_text: Mira and Amit jog on a circular park trail starting from the same point at the same time. If they jog in the same direction, in 30 minutes Amit completes exactly 3 more rounds than Mira. If they jog in opposite directions, they meet for the first time exactly 2 minutes after starting. The number of rounds Mira completes in one hour is:

Mohan, Naveen, and Omar run clockwise on a circular trail of length 2 km, all starting from the same point at the same time. Their speeds are 20 km/hr, 12 km/hr, and 8 km/hr respectively. The distance, in km, that Omar will have run when Mohan and Naveen meet again for the first time at the starting point is:

Pradeep, Qutub, and Rashmi cycle along a straight road in the same direction at constant speeds of 20 km/hr, 30 km/hr, and 60 km/hr respectively. Qutub starts 4 hours after Pradeep. If Qutub and Rashmi overtake Pradeep at the same instant, the number of hours after Pradeep that Rashmi started is:

Ramesh and Suresh walk around a circular jogging track of circumference 1800 metres. Ramesh walks at 150 metres per minute and Suresh at 100 metres per minute. If they start from the same point and walk in the same direction, after how many minutes will they be together again?

Aman and Bhanu walk on a circular track of circumference 1500 metres. When they walk in opposite directions starting from the same point, they meet every 3 minutes. When they walk in the same direction starting from the same point, Aman overtakes Bhanu every 15 minutes. Aman's speed, in km/hr, is:
In a 100-metre sprint, Anil beats Bipin by 25 metres and beats Charan by 40 metres. By how many metres will Bipin beat Charan in a 100-metre race between just the two of them?

In an 800-metre charity walk-and-run event, Tara gives Veer a head start of 300 metres and still beats him by 20 seconds. If Veer's speed is 5 metres per second, Tara's speed, in metres per second, is:

Hari, Jagat, and Kabir run on a circular track of length 600 metres, all starting from the same point and running in the same direction at uniform speeds of 5 m/s, 3 m/s, and 2 m/s respectively. The number of minutes after the start when all three are simultaneously at the starting point for the first time is:
In a sprint race, athlete Anita beats athlete Bina by 60 metres, and Bina beats athlete Charu by 80 metres. If Anita beats Charu by 120 metres, the length of the race, in metres, is:

Asha and Bharat run on a straight road from opposite ends A and B towards each other. After they cross each other, Asha takes 9 minutes to reach B and Bharat takes 16 minutes to reach A. If they started simultaneously, the ratio of Asha's speed to Bharat's speed is:

Two circular cycling velodromes, T1 and T2, with radii 150 metres and 50 metres respectively, touch externally at a point P. Cyclist Pooja rides on T1 at 12 km/hr and cyclist Quentin rides on T2 at 5 km/hr, both starting from P at the same instant. The number of complete rounds Pooja makes before they meet again at P for the first time is:
In a three-horse race, the Stallion beats the Filly by 15 metres and the Colt by 100 metres. If the Filly beats the Colt by 88 metres, the length of the racecourse, in metres, is:

Tom jogs on track P at 8 km/hr and Linda jogs on track Q at 6 km/hr. The total length of tracks P and Q together is 430 metres. While Tom completes 9 rounds of track P, Linda completes 4 rounds of track Q. In how many seconds will Linda complete one round of track P?

In a 5-km cycling race, Indira, Jaya, and Kavita each ride at uniform speeds and finish first, second, and third respectively. If Indira beats Jaya by 1 km and Jaya beats Kavita by 1 km, then by how many metres does Indira beat Kavita?

Sahil and Tara race between points P and Q, 12 km apart. Sahil starts at 6:00 a.m. from P at 6 km/hr, reaches Q, and immediately returns to P at the same speed. Tara starts at 7:30 a.m. from P at 12 km/hr, reaches Q, and immediately returns to P at the same speed. The distance, in km, from P at which Sahil and Tara meet for the first time is:

Aakash and Bhumi run a 500-metre race in which Aakash beats Bhumi by 50 metres. To do a favour to Bhumi, Aakash starts 50 metres behind the starting line in a second 500-metre race; both run at their earlier speeds. Which of the following is true about the second race?
options: Aakash and Bhumi finish together
Bhumi beats Aakash by 5 metres
Aakash beats Bhumi by 5 metres
Aakash beats Bhumi by 50 metres

question_text: In a 5400-metre cycling time-trial around a circular velodrome of circumference 1500 metres, the fastest cyclist and the slowest cyclist are at the same point on the track at the end of the 5th minute, for the first time after the start. All cyclists start from the same point and maintain uniform speeds. If the fastest cyclist's speed is thrice the slowest's, the time, in minutes, taken by the fastest cyclist to complete the race is:

In a cross-country race, runner Aanya finishes 15 metres ahead of Bhavna and 25 metres ahead of Charuvi. Bhavna finishes 12 metres ahead of Charuvi. Each runner travels at a constant speed. The length of the race, in metres, is:

In an 800-metre race, Pravin gives Quoran a head start of 80 metres so that the two finish together. In a 400-metre race, Pravin gives Rohan a head start of 16 metres so that the two finish together. If Quoran and Rohan run a 1200-metre race against each other, who wins and by how many metres?
options: Quoran wins by 75 metres
Rohan wins by 60 metres
Rohan wins by 75 metres
Quoran wins by 60 metres

question_text: Priya, Quentin, and Rashmi race over 1 kilometre. Priya beats Quentin by 20 seconds. Priya beats Rashmi by 200 metres. Quentin beats Rashmi by 5 seconds. The time, in seconds, taken by Rashmi to complete the race is:

In a town, $100$ residents were polled about three transport modes they use - bus, metro and auto. Exactly $80$ use the bus, $90$ use the metro and $60$ use the auto, and some residents may use none of the three. The difference between the maximum and the minimum possible number of residents who use all three modes is:
Among $240$ viewers, $130$ enjoy action films and $150$ enjoy comedy films. Then the number of viewers who enjoy only comedy films can possibly be:

In a product survey, $82\%$ of those asked favoured at least one of three proposed features: I, II and III. Of those asked, $55\%$ favoured feature I, $38\%$ favoured feature II and $24\%$ favoured feature III. If $12\%$ of those asked favoured all three features, what percentage of those asked favoured more than one of the three features?
In a neighbourhood, $\frac{5}{8}$ of the residents own a car, $\frac{2}{5}$ own a bicycle, and $\frac{1}{4}$ own both. The fraction of residents who own at least one of a car and a bicycle is:

A sports academy enrols $180$ athletes, each of whom trains in at least one of three programmes: swimming, athletics and cycling. In all, $90$ train in swimming, $132$ in athletics and $54$ in cycling. At least one athlete trains in all three programmes. The number who train in both swimming and cycling equals the number who train in both athletics and cycling, and this common number is half the number who train in both swimming and athletics. The maximum possible number of athletes who train in swimming but not athletics is:

At an arts centre, every learner must enrol in at least two of the three workshops: pottery, painting and sculpture. The number enrolled in all three workshops is $15$, the number enrolling in painting is $20$, and the number enrolling in sculpture is $27$. The smallest possible number of learners who could enrol in pottery is:

A music school has $300$ students, each of whom learns at least one of three instruments: guitar, violin and flute. In total, $170$ learn the guitar, $145$ learn the violin and $160$ learn the flute. Also, $75$ learn both guitar and violin, $40$ learn both violin and flute, and $80$ learn both guitar and flute. The number of students who learn only the violin is:

For two sets $X$ and $Y$, let $X\Delta Y$ denote the set of elements that belong to $X$ or $Y$ but not to both. Four playlists are catalogued by track numbers as $P_1=\{1,2,3,5,8\}$, $P_2=\{2,4,5,6\}$, $P_3=\{1,3,6,7,9\}$ and $P_4=\{2,3,8,10\}$. The number of elements in $(P_1\Delta P_2)\Delta(P_3\Delta P_4)$ is:

Each of $100$ participants at a workshop studies at least one of three languages: French, German and Spanish. Exactly $12$ participants study all three languages, while $28$ study both French and German but not Spanish. Every participant who studies Spanish also studies French or German (or both). If the number studying French equals the number studying German, then the number studying French is:

A library tracked readership of its monthly newsletter over three consecutive months - March, April and May - among a group of members. The records show: read only May, $20$; read May but not April, $25$; read both May and March, $12$; read May, $35$; read March, $50$; read both March and April, $18$; read none of the three months, $22$. The number of members who read exactly two consecutive issues (out of the three) is:

Anil spent a short holiday with Bharat. On each morning they could go cycling, and on each evening they could go fishing, but on any given day they did at most one of the two activities; on some days they did neither. There were $20$ mornings on which they did not cycle, $16$ evenings on which they did not fish, and on $28$ days in all they either cycled or fished. For how many days did Anil stay with Bharat?

A collector gathered $54$ marbles, each made either of glass or of clay. On sorting them she found: the number of red glass marbles that are swirled is even and positive; the number of red glass marbles that are not swirled equals the number of red clay marbles that are not swirled; every non-red glass marble is swirled, and there are five times as many of these as there are red swirled glass marbles; no swirled clay marble fails to be red; there are exactly $10$ red swirled clay marbles; and there are exactly $28$ clay marbles that are neither swirled nor red. How many glass marbles did she collect?

A survey of $250$ listeners, each of whom tunes in to at least one of three radio stations - RedFM, MirchiFM and CityFM - found that $86\%$ listen to RedFM, $24\%$ listen to MirchiFM and $14\%$ listen to CityFM. The maximum possible percentage of listeners who can listen to all three stations is:

Eighty households were surveyed about owning a microwave, an air-conditioner and a washing machine. Of them, $35$ own a microwave, $28$ own an air-conditioner and $30$ own a washing machine. Exactly $14$ households own exactly two of the three appliances, and $3$ households own all three. How many of the $80$ households own none of the three appliances?

A town has three committees, X, Y and Z, with $55$, $65$ and $90$ members respectively. Exactly $12$ people serve on all three committees, and $120$ people serve on exactly one committee. How many people serve on exactly two of the committees?

In an office park there are $130$ offices. Of these, $38$ have a parking bay, and these offices have neither a cafeteria nor a gym. The number of offices having a cafeteria only is three times the number having a gym only. The number of offices having both a cafeteria and a gym is one-third the number having a cafeteria only. The number of offices having either a cafeteria or a gym or both is $45$. How many offices have none of the three - cafeteria, gym or parking bay?

In a college, every student belongs to at least one of three societies: Photography, Trekking and Quiz. The Photography society has $110$ members, Trekking has $95$ and Quiz has $80$. Exactly $28$ students belong to both Photography and Trekking, $26$ belong to both Trekking and Quiz, and $22$ belong to both Photography and Quiz. If the number of students belonging to exactly two societies equals the number belonging to all three societies, then the total number of students in the college is:

In a company, every employee is trained in at least one of three modules: Sales, Operations and Technology. The number trained in exactly two modules is $44$, and the number trained in all three modules is $10$. The number trained in Sales is twice the number trained in Operations only. The number trained in Sales only equals the combined total of those trained in both Operations and Technology but not Sales and those trained in both Sales and Technology but not Operations. If $96$ employees are trained in Technology and $70$ are trained in Operations, find the total number of employees in the company.

In a sports club where every member plays at least one of three games - badminton, squash and table tennis - $95$ members play badminton, $85$ play squash and $60$ play table tennis. The total number playing both badminton and squash is $45$, both squash and table tennis is $30$, and both badminton and table tennis is $25$. The club has $150$ members and the number who play exactly one game equals the number who play exactly two games. Find the number of members who play all three games.

In a survey of $280$ commuters about three travel apps - A, B and C - it was found that $155$ use app A, $125$ use app B and $100$ use app C. The number of commuters using exactly two of the apps is $70$, and the number using all three apps equals the number using none of the apps. The number of commuters using all three apps is:

A library has a single checkout counter with a queue of students. Suppose the ratio of students standing ahead of Maya to students standing behind Maya is 4 : 7. If the total number of students in the queue is less than 260, the maximum possible number of students standing ahead of Maya is:

A debate team has participated in 50 inter-college rounds so far this season and won 40% of them. If they win 70% of the remaining rounds, their overall winning percentage for the season will become 60%. If instead they win 85% of the remaining rounds, the total number of rounds the team will have won by the end of the season is:

A jewellery store has 360 rings on display, each either gold or silver. The number of rings featuring gemstones is the same among gold rings as among silver rings. If 25% of the gold rings and 50% of the silver rings feature gemstones, the number of rings without gemstones in the store is:

Identical packets of dried fruit are sold in two sizes — regular and premium. The premium pack is sold for three times the price of the regular pack. If the selling price per gram of dried fruit in the premium pack is 16% less than that in the regular pack, then the percentage by which the weight of dried fruit in the premium pack exceeds that in the regular pack is nearest to:

A sum of money is divided among Priya, Quentin, and Riya. The ratio of Priya's and Quentin's shares is 5 : 4, while the ratio of Quentin's and Riya's shares is 6 : 7. If the difference between the largest and the smallest of these three shares is ₹450, then Riya's share, in rupees, is:

Aanya, Bharat, and Charu deposit their savings into three different bonds with deposit amounts in the ratio 3 : 5 : 6, earning annual interest rates in the ratio 3 : 2 : 2 respectively. What is their total annual interest income, in rupees, if Bharat's annual interest exceeds Aanya's by ₹120?

In a research lab, 75% of the team members are senior researchers and the rest are junior researchers. There are 50 more senior researchers than junior researchers. If 80% of the team, including 17 junior researchers, attended a conference, what percentage of the senior researchers did NOT attend the conference?

A delivery rider earns ₹100 per hour for daytime deliveries and ₹150 per hour for late-night deliveries. In a particular week, she worked a total of 100 hours, and her income from late-night deliveries was 50% of her income from daytime deliveries. The number of hours she spent on late-night deliveries that week was:
In a coding competition, P scored 20% less than Q, Q scored 40% more than R, and R scored 25% less than S. If P scored 252, S's score was:

Anil and Pooja initially had stickers in the ratio 5 : 11. Anil then gave some of his stickers to Pooja. As a result, the new ratio of Anil's stickers to Pooja's stickers became 1 : 3. What fraction of Anil's original stickers did he give to Pooja?

The amount of code Asha and Ben write together in a day equals what Chitra alone writes in 7 days. The amount of code Chitra and Asha write together in a day equals what Ben alone writes in 2 days. The ratio of the daily code output of the person who writes the most code per day to that of the person who writes the least is:

Sugar is sold for 4 consecutive weeks at the rates of ₹30, ₹40, ₹50, and ₹60 per kg, respectively. A household spends a fixed amount on sugar in each of the first two weeks and then double that amount on sugar in each of the next two weeks. The average price paid by the household, in rupees per kg, over the four weeks is closest to:

The total of urban and rural subscribers to a magazine grew by 30% from 2015 to 2018. Over the same period, urban subscribers grew by 50% while rural subscribers grew by 20%. From 2018 to 2021, rural subscribers grew by 25%. In 2021, rural subscribers were twice the urban subscribers. The percentage increase in the total number of subscribers from 2015 to 2021 is:

A canteen has two cost components per month: one part is fixed, and the other is proportional to the number of subscribers it serves. Each subscriber pays ₹2,000 per month. When the canteen has 40 subscribers, the profit is ₹300 per subscriber. When it has 60 subscribers, the profit is ₹450 per subscriber. When the number of subscribers is 80, the total profit of the canteen, in rupees, will be:

A coffee shop offers coffee in cups of three different sizes. The product of the prices, in rupees, of the three different sizes equals 540. The prices of the smallest and medium sizes are in the ratio 1 : 2. If the shop owner increases the prices of the smallest and medium cups by ₹3 each while keeping the price of the largest size unchanged, the new product of the three prices becomes 1,620. The sum of the original prices of the three different sizes, in rupees, is:

A speaker delivered (n + 3) talks across a lecture series and recorded the audience attendance at each talk. The average audience size across all (n + 3) talks was 28. In the last three talks, the audiences were 38, 18, and 13 respectively. At each of the first n talks, the audience size was less than 38. The average audience size across those first n talks was 31, and the smallest audience size at any of those n talks was z. The smallest possible value of z is:

In a recent music competition, Tara's score was one-eighth of the combined scores of Sahil and Veer. After the judges' reassessment, each contestant's score was increased by 4 points. The reassessed scores of Veer, Sahil, and Tara were in the ratio 8 : 5 : 2. By how many points did Veer's reassessed score exceed Tara's reassessed score?

The monthly sales of three products P, Q, and R were in the ratio 2 : 3 : 6 in 2020, and in the ratio 5 : 4 : 3 in 2023. If sales of P increased by 50% during 2020-2023, the percentage change in sales of R during the same period is closest to:

In a certification exam, the maximum possible score is M and the cutoff for clearing the exam is 60% of M. A candidate scored 48 marks but fell short of the cutoff by 80%. Which one of the following is correct?
